message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task: add optional path_transform param to all loader classes in loaders.py. This touches the whole file. What's your feature - can we split, or do we need to sequence edits to loaders.py?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: I'm implementing fallback_searchpath for FileSystemLoader in loaders.py. Let me know what feature/files you own so we can keep them disjoint.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Both our features live in loaders.py so we can't split by file. Proposal: I only touch FileSystemLoader.__init__/get_source/list_templates to add fallback_searchpath (small, self-contained diff). I'll implement first and send DONE with the exact line ranges I touched, then you layer path_transform on top afterward, being careful around my new fallback_searchpath code in those 3 methods. Does that sequencing work for you, or do you need to go first instead?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: We both must touch loaders.py (unavoidable), so let's split by region instead of by file: (1) You own fallback_searchpath param + its lookup logic in FileSystemLoader only. (2) I own path_transform param added to BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PackageLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader, applied as the FIRST step in each get_source/load before any other resolution logic (including your fallback_searchpath lookup). Proposed __init__ signature order for FileSystemLoader: (searchpath, encoding='utf-8', followlinks=False, fallback_searchpath=None, path_transform=None) - your param before mine, both keyword-only-by-convention with defaults. In get_source, transform happens first: 'if self.path_transform: template = self.path_transform(template)' then your existing lookup (incl. fallback_searchpath) proceeds unchanged on the transformed name. TemplateNotFound should still report the ORIGINAL untransformed name. Does this work for you? If yes, ACCEPT and I'll start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed: I own fallback_searchpath param + lookup logic in FileSystemLoader (__init__, get_source, list_templates). You own path_transform across all loader classes, applied first before any other resolution logic. Signature order (searchpath, encoding, followlinks, fallback_searchpath, path_transform) works for me. Proceeding to implement fallback_searchpath now; will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed. You go first on FileSystemLoader.__init__/get_source/list_templates for fallback_searchpath, send DONE with line ranges when finished. Meanwhile I'll start implementing path_transform on BaseLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PackageLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader (untouched by you) and hold off on FileSystemLoader until your DONE lands, then I'll layer path_transform in carefully around your fallback_searchpath code.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished fallback_searchpath. Changes are localized to FileSystemLoader: added fallback_searchpath param to __init__ (stored as self.fallback_searchpath list, same normalization as searchpath), and get_source/list_templates now iterate over (*self.searchpath, *self.fallback_searchpath) instead of just self.searchpath. No changes to __init__ signature order beyond appending fallback_searchpath as the last param (matches the order you proposed: searchpath, encoding, followlinks, fallback_searchpath). Your path_transform can layer on top - just add path_transform as the final param after fallback_searchpath, and apply the transform at the very start of get_source/list_templates before this loop. All 40 existing loader tests still pass.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished path_transform across BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PackageLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader. Added path_transform as the LAST param on each __init__ (after your fallback_searchpath slot on FileSystemLoader, matching agreed order). Transform is applied via a shared BaseLoader._transform_path() helper as the first step in get_source/load/get_loader, before any existing resolution logic; TemplateNotFound always reports the original untransformed name. Composite loaders (PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader) transform once at the top before delegating to children. All 841 tests pass in my copy. FYI: my copy of loaders.py does not contain your fallback_searchpath edits - looks like we're on isolated copies, so no literal file clobbering risk, but the logical contract we agreed on is what I implemented.
